gpt-5.5.geometry_accuracy_analysis: The scene constructs a real 3D orthographic-projection setup: a grey projection plane, a stepped/block object with actual depth, dashed parallel projection rays, global X/Y/Z axes, image-plane x/y axes, labels, and a colored projected image on the plane. The major topology is recognizable and not fake 3D, but the projected colored footprint does not fully match the 3D object's X-Y extent, and some axis/label placements are approximate rather than tightly source-matched.
gpt-5.5.geometry_accuracy_reasoning: All major geometric elements are present as true 3D objects with plausible relationships, but the projection shape and some placements have noticeable minor-to-moderate mismatches.
gpt-5.5.interactivity_usability_analysis: The code includes OrbitControls for rotate/zoom, but this does not count as meaningful interaction. A visible compact Depth button cycles near/far/farther states, animating the object along Z, updating dashed projection rays, highlighting the object, and showing an explanatory popup; this visibly functions and is pedagogically useful. The scene also registers hover/click interactions on the projection plane, projected image, 3D object, and projection rays: hover shows tooltips and click opens explanatory popups, with the object click additionally flashing a highlight; these are functional concept explanations, though thin dashed rays may be harder to select. Escape hides popups, and a Reset button has a handler but is hidden so it is not practically usable. The UI is small in the lower-left corner and does not cover important geometry; the top-right popup is modest and nonintrusive.
gpt-5.5.interactivity_usability_reasoning: There are 3+ functional pedagogical interactions including a step-through depth animation and multiple click/hover explanations, with compact UI that does not obscure the figure.
gpt-5.5.faithfulness_analysis: The rendered scene contains the same broad concept—axes, a projection plane, dashed projection lines, and colored 3D blocks—but the default view is much more zoomed out and compressed, with the entire diagram occupying a small central area instead of the source's large, edge-to-edge textbook composition. The camera angle, aspect ratio, block proportions, plane size/crop, line weights, label sizes/positions, and whitespace differ substantially, and there is visible UI chrome at the bottom left.
gpt-5.5.faithfulness_reasoning: Although recognizable in theme, the crop, zoom, proportions, and composition match well under 65% of the source, so it is not a faithful drop-in replacement.
gpt-5.5.label_quality_analysis: Source labels: uppercase Y on the vertical world axis, uppercase X on the diagonal world axis, uppercase Z on the horizontal world axis, lowercase y on/near the projection-plane vertical direction, lowercase x on/near the projection-plane slanted direction, and the text “Projection plane” below the plane. In the generated figure all six labels appear with correct text and are readable, but the lowercase x/y and especially “Projection plane” are much smaller than in the source; “Projection plane” is also placed close to the plane/axis rather than prominently below it. The uppercase axis labels are mostly clear, though their relative size and placement do not fully match the source’s large textbook-style labels.
gpt-5.5.label_quality_reasoning: All labels are present and legible, but several have significant font-size and placement mismatches relative to the source, especially the projection-plane label and the lowercase x/y labels.
gpt-5.5.concept_accuracy_analysis: The figure is intended to illustrate orthographic projection: a 3D stepped block projects onto an X-Y image plane by parallel rays along Z, with depth changes not altering image size. The tooltips and popup text are mostly figure-specific and correctly mention parallel rays, the projection plane, x = X and y = Y, and the contrast with perspective projection. However, the projected colored footprint on the plane does not match the full X-Y extent of the 3D block or the ray endpoints, and the green image-plane x-axis is drawn diagonally in X-Y despite the explanation that image coordinates share X/Y directions. Overall the main concept is present and well explained, but there are notable geometric inconsistencies in the projected shape and axes.
gpt-5.5.concept_accuracy_reasoning: The core orthographic-projection idea and explanations are specific and mostly correct, but the projection footprint and image-axis geometry contain multiple inaccuracies, so this fits the rubric for a score of 3.
